verses.text: Secure on his throne, David asks, 'Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan's sake?' He seeks out the family of his old enemy, not for revenge, but to keep a covenant of love. Grace goes looking for someone to bless.

Secure on his throne, David asks, 'Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan's sake?' He seeks out the family of his old enemy, not for revenge, but to keep a covenant of love. Grace goes looking for someone to bless.

Secure on his throne, David asks, 'Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan's sake?' He seeks out the family of his old enemy, not for revenge, but to keep a covenant of love. Grace goes looking for someone to bless.

Secure on his throne, David asks, 'Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan's sake?' He seeks out the family of his old enemy, not for revenge, but to keep a covenant of love. Grace goes looking for someone to bless.

Lame Mephibosheth is brought, terrified, expecting death — but David restores all Saul's land and seats him at the king's table as a son. Mephibosheth marvels he is noticed at all: 'a dead dog such as I.' So grace stoops to the helpless and seats the unworthy at the King's table.

Lame Mephibosheth is brought, terrified, expecting death — but David restores all Saul's land and seats him at the king's table as a son. Mephibosheth marvels he is noticed at all: 'a dead dog such as I.' So grace stoops to the helpless and seats the unworthy at the King's table.

Lame Mephibosheth is brought, terrified, expecting death — but David restores all Saul's land and seats him at the king's table as a son. Mephibosheth marvels he is noticed at all: 'a dead dog such as I.' So grace stoops to the helpless and seats the unworthy at the King's table.

Lame Mephibosheth is brought, terrified, expecting death — but David restores all Saul's land and seats him at the king's table as a son. Mephibosheth marvels he is noticed at all: 'a dead dog such as I.' So grace stoops to the helpless and seats the unworthy at the King's table.

David appoints Ziba and his household to farm the land for Mephibosheth, providing for him fully. Kindness here is not a passing gesture but a settled provision. True grace not only welcomes but makes lasting arrangement for the one it has taken in.

David appoints Ziba and his household to farm the land for Mephibosheth, providing for him fully. Kindness here is not a passing gesture but a settled provision. True grace not only welcomes but makes lasting arrangement for the one it has taken in.

David appoints Ziba and his household to farm the land for Mephibosheth, providing for him fully. Kindness here is not a passing gesture but a settled provision. True grace not only welcomes but makes lasting arrangement for the one it has taken in.

Mephibosheth, still 'lame on both his feet,' dwells in Jerusalem and eats continually at the king's table. His lameness remains, yet he feasts as a son. So those whom grace receives keep their weakness, but are honoured and fed at the King's own table — a picture of the gospel.
